title: OpenTelemetry Quiz
description: Test your knowledge on Spans, Traces, and vendor-agnostic observability.

questions.question: What primary problem in a microservices architecture does Distributed Tracing solve that standard metrics cannot?

options.text: It tracks the history of developer commits in Git.
It pinpoints exactly which microservice in a long chain of requests caused a bottleneck or error.
It automatically encrypts network traffic.
It acts as a high-availability Load Balancer.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of standardizing your application code using the OpenTelemetry API?

options.text: It makes your application code immune to security vulnerabilities.
It guarantees 100% uptime for all microservices.
It writes the telemetry data directly into the kernel memory.
It provides a vendor-agnostic way to collect data; allowing you to switch analysis platforms (like migrating from Jaeger to DataDog) without modifying application code.

questions.question: In OpenTelemetry, what term describes a single, timed unit of work, such as a single database query or a function call?

options.text: A Route
A Trace
A Span
A Log Entry

questions.question: How do multiple decoupled microservices link their work together to form a single continuous Trace?

options.text: They all log to the exact same local text file on the server.
By communicating via gRPC exclusively.
They execute on the exact same Docker host.
By injecting and propagating a unique 'Trace Context' (Trace ID) through HTTP headers or message metadata as the request travels between services.

questions.question: What does the 'Auto-Instrumentation' feature of OpenTelemetry agents (like in Java or Node.js) allow you to do?

options.text: It automatically rewrites inefficient backend SQL queries.
It automatically intercepts standard library calls, generating spans for HTTP requests and database queries without requiring manual coding.
It automatically triggers the CI/CD pipeline upon errors.
It writes unit tests for your code.
